title: Building a git url from a tag
body:
**Description**

Prior to #4149, the build url could target a specific tag in a repository. This is because before that PR, the repository was cloned with `git clone <url> -b <ref>`, which works with tags. But now, it uses `git fetch --depth=1 origin -- <tag>`, which doesn't actually create the local tag without `--tags`, so you cannot check it out.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```bash
buildah build --layers -t gittarget -f tests/bud/shell/Dockerfile https://github.com/containers/buildah.git\#v1.11.0
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```bash
> buildah build --layers -t gittarget -f tests/bud/shell/Dockerfile https://github.com/containers/buildah.git\#v1.11.0
error prepping temporary context directory: cloning "https://github.com/containers/buildah.git#v1.11.0" to "/var/tmp/buildah470663880":
error: pathspec 'v1.11.0' did not match any file(s) known to git
: failed while performing `git checkout`: exit status 1
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

The same as building a branch:

```bash
> buildah build --layers -t gittarget -f tests/bud/shell/Dockerfile https://github.com/containers/buildah.git\#release-1.11-rhel
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: SHELL [ "/bin/sh", "-c" ]
--> Using cache ece264a0b804705ad7df6dd1d559a2f2ac6bc67b99b0f8150ff2af5fa7fd98ce
COMMIT gittarget
--> ece264a0b80
Successfully tagged localhost/gittarget:latest
ece264a0b804705ad7df6dd1d559a2f2ac6bc67b99b0f8150ff2af5fa7fd98ce
```


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.27.3-1.module+el8.7.0+1122+04a7498b.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.27.3
Go Version:      go1.18.4
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.22.1
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan 12 11:32:23 2023
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Rocky Linux release 8.7 (Green Obsidian)
NAME="Rocky Linux"
VERSION="8.7 (Green Obsidian)"
ID="rocky"
ID_LIKE="rhel centos fedora"
VERSION_ID="8.7"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="Rocky Linux 8.7 (Green Obsidian)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;32"
LOGO="fedora-logo-icon"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:rocky:rocky:8:GA"
HOME_URL="https://rockylinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.rockylinux.org/"
ROCKY_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Rocky-Linux-8"
ROCKY_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="8.7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Rocky Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="8.7"
Rocky Linux release 8.7 (Green Obsidian)
Rocky Linux release 8.7 (Green Obsidian)
Rocky Linux release 8.7 (Green Obsidian)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux CL1277 5.15.79.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Wed Nov 23 01:01:46 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
> grep -v -e '^$' -e '^#' /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "false", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

comments.body: More information, I'm guessing unconditionally applying `-t` is undesirable since it will fetch all tags, defeating the purpose of `--depth=1`. You can fetch a single tag by running `git fetch origin tag <tag>`, shortcut for `git fetch origin refs/tags/<tag>:refs/tags/<tag>`, but you would have to know it's a tag before hand.

`docker build` can take a tag or a branch using `#` as a separator. Commits are not supported (same behaviour as `buildah` before #4149). Reference: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/build/#git-repositories

My guess is that docker does `clone -b` just like podman did before, which explains why it doesn't support commits. `buildah` could try and detect if the `#` part is a commit (hexadecimal + length check but that'd be brittle), or use a new syntax like you proposed.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc this is waiting for your response.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
